testcases.title: Render the complete Devin settings page
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.

steps.action: Open Enterprise Settings → Devin in a cold page load.
Inspect every settings section and the browser console.

assertions: Model and mode controls render.
Native deployments, web search, git attribution, commit email, lock email, and PR behavior controls render.
The page has no blocking UI or console errors.

testcases.title: Explain current, disabled, and locked control states
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.

steps.action: Inspect every switch, dropdown, value, label, and description without saving.
Inspect controls that are disabled or locked by another setting or policy.

assertions: Every control exposes its current value and purpose.
Disabled and locked controls explain why they cannot be changed.
Inspection does not mutate settings.

testcases.title: Persist or safely reject model and mode changes
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.
The original model and mode settings have been recorded.

steps.action: Change each available Ultra, Fast, SWE, GPT, and Fusion setting one at a time.
Save the change and record the response.
Reload the page and inspect the effective value.

assertions: Each accepted change persists after reload.
Each rejected change shows an actionable error and preserves the prior value.
One setting change does not unexpectedly alter unrelated settings.

steps: Restore every changed model and mode value.
Reload and verify the original configuration is active.

testcases.title: Apply tool and deployment settings to new sessions
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.
The original native deployments and web search values have been recorded.
Creating a disposable session is approved.

steps.action: Change native deployments and web search independently.
Save and reload the settings page.
Create a disposable session and inspect the tools available to it.

assertions: Accepted settings persist after reload.
New sessions receive tools according to the saved enterprise settings.
The setting change does not expose disallowed tools.

steps: Restore the original native deployments and web search values.
Reload and verify the original values are active.

testcases.title: Persist git commit attribution selection
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.
The original commit attribution value has been recorded.

steps.action: Open the commit attribution control and inspect every available option.
Select and save each option in turn.
Reload after each save and inspect the effective selection.

assertions: Each accepted selection persists after reload.
Option descriptions match the effective git metadata behavior.
Enterprise policy overrides are explicit.

steps: Restore the original commit attribution value.
Reload and verify the original value is active.

testcases.title: Validate custom git commit email values
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.
The original commit email setting has been recorded.

steps.action: Test default and valid custom email values.
Test blank, malformed, long, Unicode, and injection-like values.
Reload after each accepted save.

assertions: Valid email values persist after reload.
Invalid values are rejected server-side with an actionable error.
Unsafe input remains inert in the UI and network response.

steps: Restore the original commit email setting.
Reload and verify the original value is active.

testcases.title: Persist the Open PRs behavior
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.
The original Open PRs setting has been recorded.

steps.action: Change the Open PRs option.
Save and reload the settings page.
Inspect a later approved PR creation flow.

assertions: The saved option persists after reload.
Later PR creation follows the saved behavior.
The UI and effective behavior remain consistent.

steps: Restore the original Open PRs value.
Reload and verify the original value is active.

testcases.title: Reject unauthorized Devin settings updates
preconditions: A non-admin QA account or approved authorization fixture is available.
The target enterprise settings have been recorded.

steps.action: Attempt to update Devin settings as a non-admin.
Attempt the update with a tampered enterprise identifier.
Reload as an administrator and inspect the effective settings.

assertions: The server rejects unauthorized setting changes.
Tampered enterprise identifiers cannot change another tenant.
The effective settings remain unchanged.

executors.blockedReason: A deterministic non-admin fixture and safe tenant-tampering harness do not exist yet.

testcases.title: Apply git and PR settings to an end-to-end PR flow
preconditions: An enterprise administrator is authenticated in the target organization.
Creating a disposable session and PR is approved.
The original git attribution, commit email, and PR settings have been recorded.

steps.action: Set an approved disposable git attribution, commit email, and PR behavior combination.
Save and reload the settings page.
Create a disposable session that commits a change and opens a PR.
Inspect the resulting commit and PR metadata.

assertions: The commit author and email follow the saved settings.
The PR opens according to the saved enterprise behavior.
No unrelated enterprise setting changes.

steps: Close or remove the disposable PR and branch according to the approved test procedure.
Reload and verify the original configuration is active.
